message:
feat(session-types): implement transport-backed session channels (G1/G2/G3)

Add mpsc-backed transport bridge for session-typed channels. Session
operations now actually send/receive values over channels instead of
purely tracking typestate transitions.

- Add SessionTransport, SessionMessage, SessionError types
- Add async methods: send_async, recv_async, select_*_async, offer_async
- Add new_transport_pair() factory for any protocol
- Add TransportBridgeContract documenting the chosen design
- Disarm drop-bomb on transport errors (broken session, not leaked)
- 5 new unit tests + 5 integration tests (62 total in session suite)
- Update module docs: transport modes, supported scope, unsupported scope

Beads: v2ofj7.7.1 (G1), v2ofj7.7.2 (G2), v2ofj7.7.6 (G3), v2ofj7.7 (Track G)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.1","title":"[STUB][F1] Define remote execution protocol, handles, and capability contract","description":"## Background
`src/remote.rs` currently exposes a remote execution story without a clear transport protocol, handle model, or capability contract. This bead defines the actual supported model.

## Technical approach
- Define handle lifetimes, message/state transitions, cancellation, and close semantics.
- Define how remote work participates in regions, obligations, and capability flow.
- Bound scope so later implementation is concrete rather than speculative.

## Test / validation plan
- Design review against region-close, loser-drain, and no-obligation-leak invariants.
- Ensure each later F-track test maps back to an explicit semantic rule from this bead.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remote protocol and handle semantics are explicit.
2. Region/cancellation/obligation interactions are defined.
3. F2-F3 can implement and validate against this contract directly.","notes":"The optimal outcome is a narrow honest remote model, not a vague promise of future distributed magic.

Downstream validation contract:
- This decision bead must leave its downstream implementation beads with explicit requirements for comprehensive unit tests, deterministic E2E or integration scripts, and structured validation logs.
- Any scope or contract decision made here must be reflected in those downstream validation obligations so later implementers do not have to infer the test story.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:10.683526840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:12:09.851446604Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:12:09.850942207Z","close_reason":"Completed: froze the remote execution contract by making src/remote.rs the authoritative F-track contract surface and aligning docs/integration.md with the existing transport-agnostic protocol, handle lifecycle, capability gating, idempotency, lease rules, and deterministic no-runtime fallback. Removed stale 'Phase 0 handle-only' / tmh.1.2 language so F2-F3 can implement against the documented contract directly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:29:47.340261396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:10.683526840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.2","title":"[STUB][F2] Implement remote spawn/result/cancel/close lifecycle","description":"## Background
With the protocol frozen, the actual remote spawn/result/cancel/close paths need to become real runtime behavior instead of phase-0 surface area.

## Technical approach
- Implement the transport-backed lifecycle defined in F1.
- Thread region ownership, cancellation, and obligation tracking through remote work.
- Fail closed on unsupported states rather than silently falling back.

## Test / validation plan
- Targeted lifecycle tests: spawn, join/result, cancel, close.
- Invariant tests for quiescence and obligation cleanup.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remote lifecycle operations are real and transport-backed under the chosen contract.
2. Cancellation and quiescence semantics are preserved across the remote boundary.
3. Unsupported states fail explicitly.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover the changed happy paths, edge cases, boundary conditions, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
5. Deterministic E2E or integration script coverage exists for the user-visible or cross-component workflow changed by this bead, with structured logs capturing `scenario_id`, feature/profile flags, seed or fixture id, config snapshot, exact command, expected outcome, observed outcome, and artifact or replay pointers.","notes":"Keep the implementation aligned with Asupersync's invariants. Remote work is not an excuse to bypass structured concurrency.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:12.096252144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:15:14.487314301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:12.096252144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:29:48.113567452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.3","title":"[STUB][F3] Build deterministic remote harness and failure-injection validation","description":"## Background
Remote execution is especially likely to regress in edge cases. This bead builds the deterministic harness and failure-injection coverage needed to keep the new contract reliable.

## Technical approach
- Add deterministic harness coverage for remote lifecycle flows.
- Add failure-injection cases for disconnects, cancellation races, and protocol violations.
- Ensure replayable evidence exists for failures.

## Test / validation plan
- Deterministic harness tests.
- Failure-injection scenarios with reproducible seeds/artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remote execution has deterministic coverage for supported lifecycle flows.
2. Failure modes are tested and reproducible.
3. Track Z can rely on this bead for remote validation evidence.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover the changed happy paths, edge cases, boundary conditions, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
5. Deterministic E2E or integration script coverage exists for the user-visible or cross-component workflow changed by this bead, with structured logs capturing `scenario_id`, feature/profile flags, seed or fixture id, config snapshot, exact command, expected outcome, observed outcome, and artifact or replay pointers.","notes":"This bead is the guardrail against rebuilding a half-real remote system that only works on the happy path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:43.642794197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:23.711473956Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:43.642794197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:29:48.886103617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7","title":"[STUB][Track G] Session-type runtime realization","description":"## Background
The session-type layer currently advances typestate but does not actually send or receive over any transport. That makes the API look stronger than the runtime behavior.

## Scope
- Choose the transport/capability bridge for session channels.
- Implement real send/recv semantics.
- Validate typed protocol behavior end-to-end.

## Why this matters
Session types are only meaningful if the runtime behavior matches the protocol progress they claim to represent.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session-type channels no longer act as typestate-only echo stubs.
2. Transport behavior, capability requirements, and close/error semantics are explicit.
3. Integration tests prove protocol progress over the real chosen bridge.","notes":"Keep the track focused on making the existing API honest. If a narrower initial transport bridge is the right step, document that explicitly rather than pretending the surface is generic before it really is.

Validation charter:
- Every child implementation bead in this track must finish with comprehensive unit coverage for happy-path, edge-case, boundary, and failure-path behavior.
- Every child implementation bead that changes runtime-observable behavior must also land at least one deterministic E2E or integration script that exercises the real workflow and the most important failure modes.
- Validation artifacts for this track must emit structured logs with, at minimum: `scenario_id`, touched feature/profile flags, seed or fixture id, config snapshot, exact command, expected outcome, observed outcome, and artifact/replay pointers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:21:36.189172857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:05.501520950Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:21:36.189172857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7.1","title":"[STUB][G1] Choose the session-type transport and capability bridge","description":"## Background
The session-type API cannot become real until it has a concrete runtime bridge. This bead chooses how session channels bind to transport/capability machinery.

## Technical approach
- Decide the transport abstraction and capability requirements for session channels.
- Define send/recv/close/error semantics at the runtime boundary.
- Bound the first supported bridge clearly rather than pretending universal transport support.

## Test / validation plan
- Check the chosen bridge against typestate promises and runtime invariants.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session-type runtime bridge is explicit.
2. Capability and lifecycle requirements are frozen.
3. G2-G3 have a concrete target contract.","notes":"The clever move here is explicit scope control. A narrower real bridge beats a generic fake one.

Downstream validation contract:
- This decision bead must leave its downstream implementation beads with explicit requirements for comprehensive unit tests, deterministic E2E or integration scripts, and structured validation logs.
- Any scope or contract decision made here must be reflected in those downstream validation obligations so later implementers do not have to infer the test story.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:45.118524810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:45:38.901533649Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:45:38.901166226Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:29:49.647633211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:45.118524810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Technical approach
- Implement real send/recv behavior over the G1 bridge.
- Handle close/error/cancellation semantics explicitly.
- Preserve typestate soundness while aligning runtime behavior with protocol progress.

 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7.3","title":"[STUB][G3] Add typed session integration coverage and contract docs","description":"## Background
After G2, the repo needs proof that typed protocol progress actually survives integration usage and that docs/examples stop over-claiming.

## Technical approach
- Add typed session integration coverage over the supported bridge.
- Update docs/examples to describe the actual supported session runtime model.
- Capture unsupported scope explicitly.

## Test / validation plan
- Deterministic integration tests for representative protocol flows.
- Documentation review against the implemented contract.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Integration coverage proves typed session flows over the chosen bridge.
2. Docs/examples match the final runtime contract.
3. Unsupported scope is explicit rather than implicit.","notes":"This bead ensures the session-type track lands as a trustworthy feature, not just an internal implementation patch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:47.957777211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:09:43.497552497Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:09:43.497176668Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of asupersync-v2ofj7.7.6; canonical G3 keeps the wired dependency graph","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:47.957777211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:05:54.696384721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8","title":"[STUB][Track H] Reactor surface truthfulness and stale-shim cleanup","description":"## Background
Not every reactor-related audit hit means "implement a missing reactor". Some are honest cfg-off unsupported surfaces; others are stale duplicate modules or legacy compatibility shims that currently misrepresent the supported surface area.

## Scope
- Freeze the public reactor support matrix.
- Resolve the legacy `UringReactor` shim.
- Reconcile duplicate or stale reactor files/docs/waivers.

## Why this matters
Reactor surface confusion turns portability and runtime-selection behavior into guesswork for contributors and downstream users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Reactor modules clearly distinguish supported implementations, cfg-off unsupported exports, and deprecated/stale compatibility shims.
2. Duplicate or misleading reactor surface area is reconciled.
3. Docs/tests/waivers match the actual reactor support matrix.","notes":"This track intentionally narrows the earlier audit claim: `windows.rs` and `kqueue.rs` already contain real implementations. The real work is truthfulness, duplicate reconciliation, and legacy-shim resolution.

Validation charter:
- Every child implementation bead in this track must finish with comprehensive unit coverage for happy-path, edge-case, boundary, and failure-path behavior.
- Every child implementation bead that changes runtime-observable behavior must also land at least one deterministic E2E or integration script that exercises the real workflow and the most important failure modes.
- Validation artifacts for this track must emit structured logs with, at minimum: `scenario_id`, touched feature/profile flags, seed or fixture id, config snapshot, exact command, expected outcome, observed outcome, and artifact/replay pointers.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:21:37.556493034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:15:38.292971990Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:15:38.292631057Z","close_reason":"All children complete: H1 (support matrix frozen), H2 (UringReactor deprecated as alias), H3 (IoUringReactor cfg-off verified honest), H4 (docs reconciled).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:21:37.556493034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8.1","title":"[STUB][H1] Freeze reactor support matrix and honest public API contract","description":"## Background
The reactor audit surfaced three distinct cases: real implementations (`kqueue.rs`, Windows IOCP), honest cfg-off unsupported exports (`io_uring.rs`), and misleading/stale surfaces (`uring.rs`, overlapping `macos.rs` docs/module story). This bead freezes the public support matrix.

## Technical approach
- Enumerate the supported reactor surface by platform/feature.
- Decide what symbols remain public, what is deprecated or internal-only, and what must be documented as unsupported.
- Use that matrix to drive H2-H4.

## Test / validation plan
- Cross-check the matrix against actual module exports and cfgs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Reactor support matrix is explicit by platform/feature.
2. Public symbol expectations are frozen.
3. H2-H4 have a concrete truthfulness target.","notes":"The key optimization is to stop treating all unsupported code as equally problematic. Only misleading or stale surfaces need invasive cleanup.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:49.389506276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:06:01.683844574Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:58:53.643404203Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of asupersync-v2ofj7.8.5; canonical H1 keeps the wired dependency graph","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:06:01.682473687Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:49.389506276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8.2","title":"[STUB][H2] Resolve the legacy UringReactor public shim","description":"## What

src/runtime/reactor/uring.rs is a legacy public shim that always returns Unsupported.
It provides a UringReactor type that delegates everything to Err(Unsupported) with the
message "UringReactor legacy shim is unsupported; use IoUringReactor".

Current code: ~150 lines of boilerplate that exists only to prevent compile errors
for old call sites that referenced UringReactor instead of IoUringReactor.

## Why

- Misleading public type: UringReactor appears in the API as if it's functional
- Zero actual users: the real io_uring reactor is IoUringReactor in io_uring.rs
- Bloats the API surface with a type that can never succeed
- The legacy shim pattern was appropriate during migration but should be retired

## Implementation

### Preferred: Deprecate and type-alias
```rust
/// Deprecated: Use [`IoUringReactor`] instead.
#[deprecated(since = "0.2.0", note = "Use IoUringReactor instead")]
pub type UringReactor = IoUringReactor;
```

This preserves compilation of any existing references while guiding migration.

### Alternative: Remove entirely
If we're confident no external consumers reference UringReactor (we have no users
per AGENTS.md), remove uring.rs entirely and update src/runtime/reactor/mod.rs.

## Testing requirements

### Unit tests
```rust
#[test]
fn legacy_uring_reactor_is_resolved() {
    // Verify one of:
    // 1. uring.rs no longer exists (fully removed)
    // 2. UringReactor is a type alias to IoUringReactor
    // 3. UringReactor has #[deprecated] attribute
    
    let uring_path = std::path::Path::new("src/runtime/reactor/uring.rs");
    if uring_path.exists() {
        let source = std::fs::read_to_string(uring_path).unwrap();
        assert!(
            source.contains("deprecated") || source.contains("type UringReactor = IoUringReactor"),
            "uring.rs exists but is neither deprecated nor a type alias"
        );
        // Must NOT contain a full struct definition with unsupported stubs
        assert!(
            !source.contains("fn new(") || source.contains("deprecated"),
            "UringReactor still has standalone method impls"
        );
        eprintln!("[PASS] UringReactor is deprecated/aliased");
    } else {
        eprintln!("[PASS] uring.rs fully removed");
    }
}
```

### Build verification
```bash
echo "[CHECK] UringReactor resolution..."
if [ -f src/runtime/reactor/uring.rs ]; then
  if rg 'deprecated' src/runtime/reactor/uring.rs -q; then
    echo "[PASS] UringReactor is deprecated"
  elif rg 'type UringReactor' src/runtime/reactor/uring.rs -q; then
    echo "[PASS] UringReactor is a type alias"
  else
    echo "[FAIL] UringReactor still has legacy shim stubs"
    exit 1
  fi
else
  echo "[PASS] uring.rs removed"
fi

cargo check --all-targets && echo "[PASS] Compiles after UringReactor changes"
```

## Acceptance criteria

- UringReactor is either removed, deprecated, or aliased to IoUringReactor
- No standalone "always returns Unsupported" stub implementation remains
- cargo check --all-targets passes
- Test added to stub_resolution_audit.rs
- H1 support matrix reflects the chosen approach

## Dependencies
- Depends on: H1 (reactor support matrix must be frozen first)
- Depended on by: H4 (stale file reconciliation)

## Parent: Track H (v2ofj7.8)","acceptance_criteria":"1. `UringReactor` no longer exists as a misleading legacy shim.
2. The chosen contract is explicit and tested.
3. Docs/re-exports match the final decision.","notes":"This is a truthfulness cleanup bead; implementation is only one of several acceptable outcomes, but perpetual misleading unsupported status is not.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:50.896557697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:06:57.607611352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:58:54.897701368Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of asupersync-v2ofj7.8.6; canonical H2 keeps the wired dependency graph","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:25:50.896557697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-v2ofj7.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:34.546481425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
